title: Rezoning approximately 266.971 acres to a Planned Industrial District
body: Adopted May 20, 2024 by a 4-1 vote. Rezones the K-Nova / Jahn Farm parcel bounded by SR 104, Durrett Road, Borror Road, and SR 762 from Exceptional Use to Planned Industrial District. Permits data centers as the only use on the property and allows utilities to be aboveground where absolutely necessary.

title: First 2024 K-Nova rezoning attempt, died for lack of motion
body: K-Nova filed a rezoning application on January 5, 2024 for the same 266.971 acres. The ordinance is stamped "Died Due to a Lack of a Motion."

source.label: Ordinance 2024-02 (PDF, died for lack of motion)

title: Adding data centers as a permitted use in the Limited Manufacturing District
body: Adopted August 7, 2023. Made data centers a permitted use in the Limited Manufacturing District by amending Chapter 1171.02(c).

title: Sewer code amended for cooling water and industrial process water discharge
body: Amended Section 1042.05 to allow the Village Administrator to consider requests and issue permits for discharge of cooling water and unpolluted industrial process waters into the sanitary sewer.

title: Annexation of approximately 267.59 acres into Commercial Point
body: Approved annexation of approximately 267.59 acres from Scioto Township into Commercial Point at the request of Eric Young as Trustee of the James R. Jahn Trust, Alice Jahn, and K-Nova LLC.

title: Prohibiting gas-fired electric generation facilities using turbines
body: Adopted February 23, 2026. Prohibits gas-fired electric generation facilities using turbines in all zoning districts. No zoning certificate, conditional use permit, variance, or other approval may be issued for such facilities.

title: Major site plan approval for Scannell Properties #627 LLC on behalf of K-Nova
body: Council approved a major site plan application filed by Scannell Properties #627 LLC on behalf of K-Nova. Same meeting recorded village wastewater capacity at 1.4 MGD with flows of 400,000 to 500,000 GPD.

title: Council minutes, February 23, 2026
body: Includes the proposed data center moratorium submitted by Council Member Miller, the adoption of Ordinance 2026-03 (gas turbine ban), Mayor Geiger's statement that the village sent non-renewal notice on the AWS NDA, and Eric Nungester's statement that the Durrett Road site allows data centers by right.

title: Council minutes, February 2, 2026
body: Includes village utility capacity numbers, the Scannell / K-Nova major site plan approval, K-Nova's CRA discussion, and an executive session on economic development and utility extension.

title: Public hearing on the second K-Nova rezoning
body: Public hearing held the same day as the adoption of Ordinance 2024-07. Tim Bivens opposed the data center site and raised concerns about power, water, police coverage, and the village's lack of a comprehensive plan.

title: Public hearing on the first 2024 K-Nova rezoning
body: M/I Homes' Josh Barkan said M/I was interested in maintaining a residential environment for Foxfire and had discussed mounds, buffering, and landscaping along the southern border. Residents raised concerns about health, environmental effects, noise, light, air, lack of registered notice, whether the public would vote, and electrical substations.

title: First K-Nova public hearing on the Jahn Farm parcel
body: Bruce Moore, representing K-Nova, discussed warehouse growth and said the requested area was zoned residential with a strip of general commercial. Public objections covered traffic, property values, wildlife, and loss of rural view. Attorney William Archer presented more than 200 signatures in opposition.

title: Statement of services for the Jahn / K-Nova annexation
body: Adopted June 5, 2023. Statement of services for the proposed 267.59-acre annexation. Commits the village to potable water, zoning, police, refuse, and street services. States the village did not then have sanitary sewer capacity available and only estimated, without guaranteeing, that capacity would be available within two years.

title: K-Nova right-of-way deed near SR 104 and Durrett Road
body: Accepted a limited warranty deed for approximately 4.308 acres from K-Nova LLC for intended right-of-way use on State Route 104 and Durrett Road. The emergency clause stated the closing between K-Nova and its potential buyer needed to be accomplished as soon as possible. The identity of the potential buyer is not stated in the resolution.

title: Committee of the Whole minutes, October 16, 2023
body: Bruce Moore (attorney for K-Nova) discussed the Jahn property annexation and zoning. K-Nova wanted PID like its property to the north and had been receiving inquiries from data centers. Moore also said he might ask Council to waive the third reading.

source.label: October 16, 2023 Committee of the Whole minutes (PDF)

title: Council minutes, November 6, 2023
body: Dustyn Fox raised the five-million-gallon-per-day water and fifty-megawatt power questions, plus an NDA reference. Bruce Moore stated his intention to ask Council to waive the third reading on the Jahn property but understood they could not.

title: Committee of the Whole minutes, March 18, 2024
body: Council raised concerns that K-Nova's attorney had been contacting council members individually. Mayor Goldhardt agreed the developer was skirting around the Sunshine Law. Solicitor Mattes warned that personal phone, text, and email communications used for village business can be public records. The same meeting included a discussion of the AWS-property TIF and stated K-Nova would still collect generated TIF money even if AWS activated the TIF.

source.label: March 18, 2024 Committee of the Whole minutes (PDF)

title: Council minutes, April 1, 2024
body: Ordinance 2024-02, the first 2024 K-Nova rezoning attempt, died for lack of a motion on its third reading. Speakers included Ron Jahn, Eric Young (executor of the Jim Jahn estate), Josh Barkan (M/I Homes), and Bruce Moore (K-Nova). Moore said the property would be used for data centers only, no warehouses.

title: Ohio Revised Code 731.17 (passage of ordinances and resolutions)
body: Village ordinances must be read on three different days unless council dispenses with that rule by at least three-fourths of its members. The vote must be by yeas and nays and entered on the journal.

title: Ohio Revised Code 731.30 (emergency ordinances)
body: Emergency ordinances go into immediate effect only if necessary for the immediate preservation of public peace, health, or safety, receive a two-thirds vote of all members elected, and state the reasons for the emergency in one section of the ordinance.

title: Ohio Revised Code 713.12 (zoning notice)
body: Zoning ordinances or amendments require a public hearing, newspaper notice at least 30 days before the hearing, and in certain small-area rezonings mailed notice to nearby owners at least 20 days before the hearing.

title: Ohio Revised Code 121.22 (Open Meetings Act)
body: Official action and deliberations must occur in open meetings unless a statutory exception applies. Minutes must be prepared and open to public inspection. Special meetings require notice of time, place, and purpose.

title: Follow-up K-Nova public hearing on revised plans and landscaping
body: K-Nova representatives discussed substitute plans, zoning text, and landscaping commitments. Residents discussed mounds, fencing, traffic lights, truck traffic, warehouse lighting, diesel smell, and continued opposition to warehouses.

title: Pickaway County minutes recording the Jahn / K-Nova annexation petition
body: Records the filing of an annexation petition for approximately 267.59 acres from Scioto Township into Commercial Point by Eric Young as Trustee of the James R. Jahn Trust, Alice N. Jahn, and K-Nova LLC. Agent: Craig Moncrief of Plank Law Firm.

title: Pickaway County approves the Jahn / K-Nova annexation petition
body: County minutes record approval of the annexation petition for approximately 267.59 acres into Commercial Point. References parcel L2700010053101 and notes that all property owners in the territory signed the petition.

title: Pickaway County minutes recording the Commercial Point annexation filing
body: Records that Commercial Point approved Ordinance 2023-11 on December 4, 2023 and filed the annexation petition with the Pickaway County Recorder on January 9, 2024.
